author: mark
text:
Wire state_branch end to end

SPEC-tracker-state §5 defines a tracker address as the pair (state_url, state_branch). bl-8a9a delivered only the resolution and materialization halves; every lifecycle refspec — claim_push, claim_sync, commands/sync, commands/half_push, commands/sync_targets, archive_recovery, remaster, remaster_detach — still named git_state::STATE_BRANCH = 'balls/tasks', so a non-default state_branch materialized a mismatched local branch while every push/fetch/merge targeted the default. bl-022c had to gate the field as 'not yet supported'.

This wires it. Store now caches the resolved state branch from .balls/state-repo's HEAD (the authoritative materialized branch) and every push/fetch/merge consumer threads through it. Read-only log_subjects calls become 'HEAD' since the state branch IS HEAD in the checkout. The warm path of Store::ensure_state_repo aligns the checked-out branch with the configured state_branch, so a 'bl remaster --branch B' and a hand-edit both re-target the existing checkout without a re-clone — best-effort so a broken state-repo stays discoverable for bl doctor.

SPEC §8: 'bl remaster <url> [--branch B]'. The CLI flag persists state_branch in .balls/config.json; --detach clears it. tracker_address::ensure_supported and its config-validate plumbing are removed (the bl-022c gate is what this ball was scoped to retire).

Conformance test (SPEC §16): tests/conformance_state_branch.rs exercises create → claim → review → close + sync round-trip on state_branch=project-x, asserting the tracker carries refs/heads/project-x and no stray refs/heads/balls/tasks, plus a second workspace cloned from the same tracker resolves the closed task via --closed (proving archive_recovery routes through HEAD, not the hardcoded default). A second test asserts 'remaster --branch B --commit' persists state_branch and aligns the state-repo HEAD, and --detach clears it.

make check is green: 673+ tests pass, clippy clean, line cap respected, 100% coverage.
